**Why it passes:** CP00-879 is a genuinely descriptor-only, runtime-closed Commercial Readiness bridge. Source inspection confirmed pure frozen-object functions with no writes/persistence/runtime receipts, synthetic-only data, fail-closed policy, comprehensive validators enforcing the no-write/no-real-data/authority-boundary invariants, tests wired into `npm test`, and an on-disk canonical contract that matches the generated projections (`validation.valid=true`, all 14 mandatory artifacts present). Manifest/command-evidence/inspection/adjudication honestly report `production_ready=false`/pending with enterprise trust deferred to human approval — no overclaim. Claude stays read-only/non-final; Hermes is evidence-metadata only, not runtime-embedded. Review-hardening preflight is clean and changed-file scope is respected.

**One finding (P3, non-blocking):** `packages/commercial/src/validators.js:954` has a vacuous self-referential loop (checks `requirements.mandatory_artifacts` against itself, so it can never fire). It's harmless dead code — real mandatory-artifact coverage exists in `validateCommercialReadinessContract`. Recommend removing or repointing it in a future cleanup.

No P0/P1/P2 issues.
